LanguageData.PSC_SettingsM1Note: More options arrive in later updates. The current release adds per-item maximums, refill thresholds, and batch hauling to stockpiles and shelves.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyFromSource: Restrict new destinations to their sources
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyFromSourceTip: When you link a source to a stockpile for the first time, automatically turn on "Only from source" for it. You can still toggle it per stockpile.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyToDestinations: Restrict new sources to their destinations
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyToDestinationsTip: When you link a destination to a stockpile for the first time, automatically turn on "Only to destinations" for it. You can still toggle it per stockpile.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsPriorityNumberingTip: Split each of the five vanilla priority bands into two levels, giving 1 (highest) to 10 (lowest). Existing stockpiles land on 1, 3, 5, 7 and 10. Turn this off to collapse back to the five bands; your letters still apply, but any feeder link that relied only on a sub-tier difference within the same band will stop working until you turn numbering back on.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsReverseOrderTip: Show the highest priority as 10 instead of 1. This only flips the displayed numbers; it does not change how stockpiles are ordered.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDebugLoggingTip: Log PSC's feeder, link, and ordering decisions to the dev console / Player.log. Only useful for diagnosing bugs; leave off for normal play.
LanguageData.PSC_LevelTip: Fine priority level (1 = highest, 10 = lowest). Two levels share each vanilla band; setting a level also sets the band.
LanguageData.PSC_LetterTip: Subpriority letter. Within the same priority, "a" outranks "b" outranks "c". No letter is highest.
LanguageData.PSC_ConnectSourceDesc: Click or drag over other stockpiles or shelves to mark them as sources that feed this storage. Items will flow from a source to this storage when this storage has the higher priority.
LanguageData.PSC_ConnectDestinationDesc: Click or drag over other stockpiles or shelves to mark them as destinations this storage feeds. Items will flow from this storage to a destination when the destination has the higher priority.
LanguageData.PSC_BreakConnectionDesc: Click or drag over other stockpiles or shelves to remove any feeder link between them and this storage. Only connections to this storage are broken; a normal click on an unconnected storage does nothing.
LanguageData.PSC_OnlyFromSourceDesc: When on, this storage only accepts items hauled from one of its linked sources — never loose or ground items, and never from unlinked storage.
LanguageData.PSC_OnlyToDestinationsDesc: When on, items in this storage are only hauled to one of its linked destinations — never to any other storage.
LanguageData.PSC_ShowConnectionsDesc: Show feeder links for every stockpile on the map (not just the selected one) while a storage is selected.
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsDesc: Remove feeder links in bulk. Right-click for two options: clear just this storage's connections, or clear every connection on the map. A normal click does nothing, to avoid clearing by accident.
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsHint: Right-click this button to clear connections.
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsConfirm: Clear all feeder connections on this map
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsConfirmDialog: Remove every feeder connection on this map? This cannot be undone.
LanguageData.PSC_BatchFillTip: Pawns won't fill this storage in a trip smaller than this many items. Items below this amount aren't hauled in until more accumulates at the source. 0 = off.
LanguageData.PSC_BatchEmptyTip: Pawns won't remove items from this storage unless they can take at least this many in one trip. Items below this amount stay until more accumulates. 0 = off.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintGlobal: Blank ends mean always refill / fill to maximum.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintItem: Blank ends mean always refill / fill to maximum. Tick marks follow stacks of {0} items.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintMixedStacks: Blank ends mean always refill / fill to maximum. Items mode is disabled because this selection contains different stack sizes.
LanguageData.PSC_ItemsModeMixedStacksTip: Items mode is disabled because this selection contains items with different stack sizes. Use stacks mode so each item keeps the same number of stacks.
LanguageData.PSC_SoftCapNote: Pawns keep each item at or below its maximum when hauling. Rarely, items spawned straight into storage (such as from a workbench standing inside the stockpile) can still exceed it.
LanguageData.PSC_SearchHint: Type in the storage tab's search box to choose which items these limits apply to.
